2606.03624.title: Bridging Auxiliary Constraints to Resolve Instruction Following in Large Reasoning Models
2606.03624.paper_summary: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in many tasks, yet they struggle with reliably following multiple instructions, either by failing to satisfy individual constraints or by struggling to balance competing constraints simultaneously. We formalize this challenge as the Constraint Adherence Problem (CAP). This paper introduces a novel framework that addresses CAP by representing instructions as a structured knowledge graph of constraints. Our approach, Constraint Relationship Graph Completion (CRGC), explicitly models relationships between constraints, identifies adherence challenges, and discovers ``bridge constraints'' that help the model better focus on and reconcile requirements. Bridge constraints act as auxiliary instructions that make primary constraints more salient and compatible. Unlike existing approaches that enhance instruction following through general training methods, CRGC specifically improves constraint satisfaction by leveraging the model's own knowledge to create better pathways for generation. Experiments across three popular instruction following datasets demonstrate that our approach reduces constraint violations by 39% compared to standard prompting while maintaining reasoning abilities of large reasoning models.
2606.03624.paper_summary_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）在許多任務中展示了令人印象深刻的能力，但它們在可靠地遵循多個指令方面仍然面臨挑戰，要麼無法滿足個別約束，要麼難以同時平衡相互競爭的約束。我們將這一挑戰正式化為約束遵循問題（CAP）。本文介紹了一個新穎的框架，通過將指令表示為結構化的約束知識圖來解決CAP。我們的方法，即約束關係圖完成（CRGC），明確建模約束之間的關係，識別遵循挑戰，並發現“橋接約束”，幫助模型更好地集中注意力並調和需求。橋接約束作為輔助指令，使主要約束更加突出和兼容。與現有的通過一般訓練方法增強指令遵循的方式不同，CRGC具體通過利用模型自身的知識來創造更好的生成路徑來改善約束滿足。對三個流行的指令遵循數據集的實驗表明，我們的方法相比於標準提示減少了39%的約束違反，同時保持了大型推理模型的推理能力。
2606.03624.author: Zhengyi Zhao et.al.
2606.03624.authors: Zhengyi Zhao, Shubo Zhang, Huimin Wang, Zezhong Wang, Yutian Zhao, Yefeng Zheng, Binyang Li, Yulan He, Kam-Fai Wong, Xian Wu
